behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is model judgment quality: whether the reply actually grounds itself in thread details. MOCK-MISS: this is not just local formatting logic. REAL-INFRA: no OS/subprocess requirement. LLM-DEP: yes; a stubbed model would hide generic/unanchored outputs. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: (Agentic) setup real LLM with a thread containing unique concrete points; assert draft mentions those points; catches real grounding failure. (Integration) setup stub model returning anchored text; assert pass; misses real model drifting to generic reply. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because behavior text describes one model decision (reply generation), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run generation against a real LLM on a thread with 2-3 unique facts (dates, commitments, named items) and assert the draft explicitly references those thread-specific facts rather than only generic pleasantries.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff between draft-claim extraction/checker and KB lookup/compare logic. MOCK-MISS: unit tests of comparator alone can miss wiring issues (claim normalization, field mapping, contradiction propagation). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required if KB is local fixture. LLM-DEP: behavior text does not require model-quality judgment for the check itself. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: (Integration) setup draft with contradictory claim plus fixture KB record; assert contradiction flag is emitted; catches parser+lookup+checker wiring bugs. (Agentic) setup real model-based checker; would catch more, but not minimally required by stated behavior. Rejection-gate: rejected Agentic because behavior text says 'check ... against a knowledge base' and does not state LLM-based adjudication quality as the requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Wire claim extraction/checker with a fixture KB and feed a draft containing a known contradiction; assert the contradiction is flagged and linked to the conflicting KB entry.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on model output behavior under missing-knowledge context: avoiding fabrication and adding disclaimer text. MOCK-MISS: deterministic stubs can always include disclaimer and hide hallucination/fabrication risk. REAL-INFRA: no special OS/subprocess need. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on real generation behavior. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: (Agentic) setup thread needing external facts with empty/no-hit KB context; assert draft contains disclaimer and avoids unsupported factual claims; catches hallucination under uncertainty. (Integration) setup stubbed generator that returns disclaimer; passes even if real model fabricates. Rejection-gate: rejected Workflow because this behavior focuses on one generation decision conditioned on KB-no-hit, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real LLM with a scenario where retrieval returns no relevant KB entries; assert the produced draft includes an uncertainty/disclaimer statement and does not introduce unverifiable concrete facts.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff between draft status, approval state, and send action. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing a single guard function can miss wiring bugs where sender is called despite pending status. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process sender spy/fake; no real SMTP/API required for minimum. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP agentic: none. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: (Integration) setup pending draft in workflow service with mocked sender adapter; assert sender not invoked until explicit approve event, then invoked once; catches orchestration bug. (System) real email API test catches transport issues but is above minimum for this gating behavior. Rejection-gate: rejected System because behavior text is about approval gating ('blocks sending until ... approves'), not transport/OS/network semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Execute workflow state transitions with a fake send adapter: verify no send call occurs before approval, and exactly one send call occurs after explicit approval.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets full pipeline execution across sequential stages with at least one real agentic decision (draft generation) and downstream handoffs (fact check, approval presentation). MOCK-MISS: stubbing model or stage boundaries can hide cross-stage failures (context loss, incorrect checker input, approval payload mismatch). REAL-INFRA: likely includes real email thread ingestion and external APIs; but even beyond System, MULTI-STEP with agentic stages makes Workflow the minimum. CANDIDATE-TEST-CHECK: (Workflow) setup real thread + real model path + KB + approval UI/service; assert sequence completes with coherent draft, fact-check result attached, and approval gate active; catches cross-stage regressions. (Agentic) isolated generation quality test cannot catch end-to-end stage handoff failures. Rejection-gate: rejected System/Agentic because behavior text explicitly requires 'full sequence ... works end-to-end,' which disqualifies single-boundary tests.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario on a real email thread through ingestion, real draft generation, factual-check stage, and approval presentation; assert each stage output is consumed by the next and final state is 'awaiting user approval' (not sent).
